commit.message:
feat: extend generator to update portal pages via marker-based injection

Portal pages (portal/index.html, portal/progress.html) were 100% hardcoded
and drifted from config.json after every generator run. This adds 14 HTML
markers and new generator functions that inject hero stats, milestones,
progress bars, timeline, task tables, and workstream cards from config data.
Idempotent -- running twice produces no diff.

Make delivery dropdown matching resilient to PPO label changes

Replace rigid regex (/Cutoff.*DELIVERY/, /DELIVERY\s*for/) with
broad pattern matching: any short element (10-100 chars) containing
a date pattern (month + day) plus delivery keywords (delivery,
cutoff, ship, arriving, etc.). Scores by SVG presence and text

fix: Security hardening, code quality, and CI improvements from project audit

- Add JWT authentication to playlist-proxy (was accepting raw userId from body)

- Remove VITE_SPOTIFY_CLIENT_SECRET fallback from server-side code

- Replace browser alert() with toast notifications in AuthModal

- Remove dead code (unused__initializeSpotifyLazy, orphaned import)

commit.message:
Bypass Tone.js entirely for custom music sessions using pure math

Generate isochronic tones, binaural beats, and music mixing with
direct sample computation instead of Tone.js offline rendering.
This eliminates the unexplained 3x amplification from Tone.js and
should fix crackling in sessions like breathe_easy.

Tone.js is still used for built-in noise sessions (white/pink/brown)
where we need the Noise generator and AutoFilter.

commit.message:
Initial commit: Ledge — programmable double-entry ledger

Complete monorepo with 5 packages (~8,600 LOC), 235 tests passing:

- @ledge/core: Double-entry engine, 8 business templates, financial
  statements (P&L, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow), CSV/OFX import with
  reconciliation, immutable transactions with reversal support
- @ledge/api: 25 REST endpoints via Hono, API key auth, cursor pagination
- @ledge/mcp: 12 MCP tools, 4 resources, 3 prompts for AI agents
- @ledge/sdk: TypeScript client SDK (560 LOC)
- @ledge/dashboard: Next.js viewer with modernized Sana Labs-inspired UI

Also includes Docker image, 3 example apps (SaaS tracker, expense
tracker, invoice manager), and comprehensive documentation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix multi-display window restore; add macro dispatch logging

Window restore: use AXRaise on the specific window via System Events
instead of `tell application id "X" to activate`, which lets macOS
pick a window on the wrong display. AXRaise forces the correct window
to the front regardless of which display it's on.

Macro dispatch: log every macro request (target app, text length,
text preview) so empty-text drops are visible in bridge output.

feat: page-based progress sync, parallel upsert, session detection in dashboard

Plugin: switch pullProgress from percentage to page-based comparison,
add forcePullAndJump menu action, auto-detect device model, enqueue
highlights/stats/session on close for faster book exit, robust WiFi
polling on resume with guard-after-success pattern.

Server: parallelize document upserts with Promise.all, detect reading
sessions from page_stats (20min gap), expose device_total_pages and
canonical_pages in calendar endpoint.

Dashboard: show real pages KPI using canonical_pages, display session
time badges in day detail, convert device pages to real pages in
day view.

fix: Move auth check before intl middleware to preserve query params on redirect

Root cause: intlMiddleware was redirecting first, stripping query params
from the URL before auth redirect could preserve them.
Fix: Check auth BEFORE running intlMiddleware for protected routes.

Two issues found from logs:
1. select_delivery_date_ppo was called AFTER proceed_to_checkout_page_ppo,
   but the delivery dropdown is on the cart page, not the review page.
   Moved the call to before navigation.
2. Element matching was hitting a sidebar container (~160 chars) instead

Fix headshots, bullpen fallbacks, and advanced metrics fallbacks

FIX 1 — Player headshots in lineup tables
- Add MLB player IDs to DEFAULT_METS_LINEUP for all 8 regular starters
- headshotImg() builds MLB CDN URL using p.playerId || 0; Cloudinary
  d_ param serves generic silhouette for missing/unknown IDs
- onerror="this.style.display='none'" silently hides any broken imgs
- Wrap name cell in .player-name-cell flex div with .player-headshot img
- Add .player-headshot and .player-name-cell to styles.css

FIX 2 — Bullpen 2025 fallbacks
- resolveBullpen(bp, isNYM, oppName) applies METS_2025 values when live
  data is null/N/A: bullpenERA, bullpenxFIP (NYM); opponents2025 bullpenERA (OPP)
- Last 14d uses bullpenERA as proxy when null, labeled (2025)

FIX 3 — Advanced metrics 2025 fallbacks
- resolveAdvancedMatchup(game) fills all 5 rows from METS_2025 when live
  values are N/A: teamWRC_plus, hardHitPct, barrelPct, bbPct, kPct
- OPP wRC+ pulled from opponents2025[oppName].teamWRC_plus
- wRC+ edge computed: "Mets" if NYM leads OPP by 5+ points, else Neutral
- Edge color logic updated to treat "N/A" same as "Neutral" (gray)
- stat2025() and fallback() helpers share (2025) span annotation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(tickets): prevent false unsaved changes warning in description editor (PUNT-353) (#427)

## Summary
- Fixes false "unsaved changes" warning when opening the description
editor without making edits
- MDXEditor normalizes markdown on initialization (e.g., trailing
newlines/whitespace), causing `tempDescription` to differ from
`ticket.description` even without user edits
- Adds a `descriptionBaselineRef` that captures the editor's first
`onChange` output as the normalized baseline, so `hasUnsavedChanges`
compares against the editor-normalized value instead of the raw ticket
description

## Test plan
- [x] Open a ticket drawer, click "Edit" on description, then press
Escape — no unsaved changes dialog should appear
- [x] Open a ticket drawer, click "Edit" on description, type something,
then press Escape — unsaved changes dialog should appear
- [ ] Open a ticket drawer, edit description and click Save — changes
should persist correctly
- [x] Open a ticket drawer, edit description and click Cancel — changes
should be discarded
- [x] Switch between tickets — description baseline should reset
properly

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: post-implementation cleanup — shared config, gpui_tokio, async init, HttpClient, thread_id fix

- Extract find_config/load_config/resolve_db_path into uglyhat::config,
  replacing 3 duplicate implementations (uh.rs, service.rs, memory.rs)
- Use gpui_tokio shared runtime instead of private tokio::runtime::Runtime
- Make UglyhatService::init non-blocking — store opens async on tokio,
  global updates when ready (callers already handle None)
- Replace curl subprocess with gpui HttpClient for cost API fetches
- Use prism_types::ThreadCostResponse instead of local ThreadCost struct

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: compute Node heap limit dynamically from system memory and concurrency

Instead of a hardcoded 1536MB, derives --max-old-space-size from
(totalMemory - 512MB reserve) / maxConcurrent, with a 1536MB floor
and 4096MB ceiling. On the 4GB production container this gives the
same 1536MB, but on larger machines (or lower concurrency) builds
automatically get more headroom. Concurrent builds are rare since
most worker time is spent on Claude API calls, so the floor is safe.

commit.message:
feat(prism-cli): Unix socket bridge for IDE approval routing

When Zed is running `prism acp`, the ACP server binds a per-project
Unix domain socket (~/.prism/run/approval-<hash>.sock). CLI sessions
(`prism run`) connect to this socket and route permission prompts
through Zed's UI instead of the terminal. Falls back to TTY if the
socket doesn't exist or the connection fails.

commit.message:
fix: default app.is_admin=true so existing code survives RLS rollout

Without this, enabling RLS with FORCE + defaulting is_admin=false would
cause all queries from existing endpoints (which don't use tenant_connection
yet) to return zero rows — bricking the entire application.

Safe rollout: admin bypass ON by default, endpoints opt-in to tenant
isolation via tenant_connection(). Flip default to false once all
endpoints are migrated.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(dashboard-e2e): operator actions E2E suite (gate advance, HTTP endpoint, disabled states)

Closes bead: agent-flywheel-console-3qw.4.3 + closes epic 3qw.4

── test/e2e/dashboard/02-operator-actions.e2e.ts (new, 14 tests) ────────

Gate advance — local action, no SSH required (5 tests):
  Demonstrates the full end-to-end round-trip through the browser dashboard:
  UI interaction → HTTP POST /action → server-side advanceGate() (now wrapped
  in a db.transaction()) → snapshot broadcast → browser DOM update.

  - Verifies gate-submit is enabled when a run exists (actionStates logic)
  - Verifies initial phase is "plan" in the metric card
  - Gate advance to "beads": select option + form submit → waits for
    "gate.advance succeeded" in #action-log → asserts #metric-phase = "beads"
  - Gate advance to "swarm": same cycle, confirms phase chain works
  - Action log: both gate advances produce INFO entries in #action-log

HTTP /action endpoint contract (3 tests):
  Uses page.evaluate() to call fetch() from within the browser's origin so
  CORS is irrelevant and the URL is always correct:
  - POST /action gate.advance → { ok: true, action: "gate.advance",
    payload: { nextPhase: "beads" } }
  - POST /action with malformed JSON → HTTP 400 + { ok: false }
  - POST /action with unknown action type → { ok: false } (no panic)

Disabled action states (3 tests):
  Empty workspace (no run, no SSH, no agents):
  - #pause-button isDisabled() = true (swarm.pause requires active agents)
  - #gate-submit isDisabled() = true (gate.advance requires a run)
  - #gate-note has non-empty guidance text (operators see why it's unavailable)

Swarm control visibility (3 tests):
  With a "swarm" phase run seeded:
  - #pause-button exists in the DOM
  - #resume-button exists in the DOM
  - #swarm-controls-note has non-empty guidance text

── Key design patterns ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

waitForActionLog(h, text): waits for a matching entry in #action-log articles,
handling the async nature of runAction() (disabled button → fetch → logAction).

All beforeAll hooks use HOOK_TIMEOUT_MS = 30s to accommodate cold Playwright
startup; individual test timeouts use the Vitest-level 30s from CLI.

Epic 3qw.4 (Browser E2E) fully closed: 00-harness (setup infrastructure),
01-readonly-states (load/empty/populated/SSH states), 02-operator-actions
(gate advance, HTTP contract, disabled state verification).

Full suite: 786 passing, 39 skipped, 0 failing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: self-host Syne font with descent-override to fix "g" clipping

Root cause: Syne's font file declares descent metrics too shallow for
the weight-700+ "g" glyph. Chrome clips at the metric boundary, cutting
off the bottom of every lowercase "g" across the site.

Fix: Remove Syne from Google Fonts <link> and self-declare @font-face
with descent-override: 30%, which tells Chrome to allocate enough
vertical space for the full descender. Includes latin + latin-ext
subsets with preload for fast loading.

This is a font-level fix, not CSS overflow — proven via raw canvas
rendering that showed the "g" clipped even with zero CSS applied.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
